entries: The giant creates a strong wind (or strong current within water). {@actSave str} {@dc 18}, each creature in a 60-foot-long, 10-foot-wide {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} originating from a point anywhere in its lair. {@actSaveFail} the target is pushed 15 feet in the direction the wind is blowing. {@actSaveSuccessOrFail} The gust disperses gas or vapor, and it extinguishes candles, torches, and similar unprotected flames in its area. Protected flames, such as those of lanterns, have a {@chance 50} chance of being extinguished.

regionalEffects: The region containing a storm giant quintessent's lair is warped by the giant's presence, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items.name: Downpour
entries: Rain, snow, or blowing dust or sand (whichever is most appropriate) is constant within 1 mile of the lair. Rain causes rivers and streams to fill or overflow their banks; snow, dust, or sand forms deep drifts or dunes.

items.name: Lightning
entries: Flashes of lightning and peals of thunder are continual, day and night, within 5 miles of the lair.

items.name: Winds
entries: High wind blows within 1 mile of the lair, making it impossible to light a fire unless the location where the fire is lit is protected from the wind.

regionalEffects: If the giant dies, the lightning, thunder, and high wind regional effects end immediately. Rain, snow, and blowing dust abate gradually within {@dice 1d8} days.

legendaryGroup.name: Yeenoghu
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), Yeenoghu can take one of the following Lair Actions; he can't take the same Lair Action two rounds in a row:

items.name: Incite the Pack
entries: Until the next {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} count 20, all gnolls and hyenas within the lair are enraged, causing them to have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on melee attack rolls and causing attack rolls to have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} against them.

items.name: Iron Spike
entries: An iron spike bursts from the ground. {@actSave dex} {@dc 24}, each creature at a point Yeenoghu can see within 100 feet. {@actSaveFail} 27 ({@damage 6d8}) Piercing damage, and the target has the {@condition restrained} condition and is impaled on the spike. A creature can use an {@variantrule action|xphb} to remove itself (or a creature it can reach) from the spike, ending the effect.

items.name: Pack Rush
entries: Each gnoll or hyena that Yeenoghu can see can use its reaction to move up to its speed.

regionalEffects: The region containing Yeenoghu's lair is warped by his magic, creating one or more of the following effects:

items.name: Savage Predators
entries: Predatory beasts within 6 miles of the lair become unusually savage, killing far more than what they need for food. Carcasses of prey are left to rot in an unnatural display of wasteful slaughter.

items.name: Spiky Terrain
entries: Within 1 mile of the lair, large iron spikes grow out of the ground and stone surfaces. Yeenoghu impales the bodies of the slain on these spikes.

items: If Yeenoghu dies, these effects fade over the course of {@dice 1d10} days.

legendaryGroup.name: Zariel
lairActions: On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), Zariel can take one of the following Lair Actions; she can't take the same Lair Action two rounds in a row:

items.name: Fireball
entries: Zariel casts the {@spell fireball} spell.

items.name: Infernal Illusions
entries: Zariel casts the {@spell major image} spell four times, targeting different areas with it. Zariel prefers to create images of intruders' loved ones being burned alive. Zariel doesn't need to {@status Concentration|XPHB|concentrate} on the spells, which end on {@variantrule initiative|xphb} count 20 of the next round. Each creature that can see these illusions is subjected to the following effect. {@actSave wis} {@dc 26}. {@actSaveFail} the target has the {@condition frightened} condition with the illusion being the source of fear. The {@condition frightened} target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. After 1 minute, it succeeds automatically.

regionalEffects: The region containing Zariel's lair is warped by her magic, which creates one or more of the following effects:

items.name: Hellscape
entries: The area within 9 miles of the lair is filled with screaming voices and the stench of burning meat.

items.name: Pyres
entries: Once every 60 feet within 1 mile of the lair, 10-foot-high gouts of flame rise from the ground. Any creature or object that touches the flame takes 7 ({@damage 2d6} Fire damage; it can take this damage no more than once per round.

items.name: Smoke
entries: The area within 2 miles of the lair, but no closer than 500 feet, is filled with smoke, which causes the area to be {@variantrule heavily obscured|xphb}. The smoke can't be cleared away.

regionalEffects: If Zariel dies, these effects fade over the course of 5 ({@dice 1d10}) days.

legendaryGroup.name: Zuggtmoy
lairActions: On {@variantrule Initiative|xphb} count 20 (losing initiative ties), Zuggtmoy can take a Lair action to cause one of the following effects; she can't use the same effect two rounds in a row:

items.name: Rally Plants
entries: Up to four plant creatures that are friendly to Zuggtmoy and that Zuggtmoy can see can use their {@variantrule reaction|xphb|reactions} to move up to their {@variantrule speed|xphb} and make one attack with a weapon it is holding.

items.name: Summon Fungi
entries: Zuggtmoy causes four {@creature gas spore||gas spores} or {@creature violet fungus||violet fungi} to appear in unoccupied spaces that she chooses within the lair. They vanish after 1 hour.

items.name: Unleash Spores
entries: Zuggtmoy uses either her Infestation Spores or her Mind Control Spores, centered on a mushroom or other fungus within her lair, instead of on herself.

regionalEffects: The region containing Zuggtmoy's lair is warped by his magic, creating one or more of the following effects:

items.name: Corrupted Nature
entries: Within 6 miles of the lair, all Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) and Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) checks have disadvantage.

items.name: Fungal Infestation
entries: Molds and fungi grow on surfaces within 6 miles of the lair, even where they would normally find no purchase.

items.name: Mutating Vegetation
entries: Vegetation within 1 mile of the lair becomes infested with parasitic fungi, slowly mutating as it is overwhelmed.

items: If Zuggtmoy dies, these effects fade over the course of {@dice 1d10} days.
